content:

This offer is by a third-party and I will be on their payroll. I know this offer is nowhere close to all other Tesco offers for the same role. But given the market condition and me searching for a job for 3 months...is it good enough to join?

title: SDE2 Offer in RMG (Nazara-backed) vs Banking Role — Worth the Switch at 4.5 YOE?
content:
Hi,

Need some advice on a switch decision.

YOE: 4.5 years
Current CTC: ~25 LPA (22 Fixed + 3 Variable)
Current Company: Banking enterprise setup

Current role:

- Stable, good WLB
- Work is slow-paced
- Limited exposure to scale / real system ownership

Offer:

Offer 1: RMG gaming company (Poker / card games space)
Role: SDE II
Total Comp: ~30 LPA

Context:

- Company is backed by Nazara Technologies
- Seems backend-heavy work (performance, concurrency, real-time systems)

Concerns:

- RMG space in India facing regulatory pressure (recent gaming laws, bans, GST issues)
- Layoffs and pivot discussions happening in some companies in this space
- Long-term stability unclear

What I’m trying to evaluate:

1. Given current market and regulations, is RMG still a good domain to enter?
2. Is ~30 LPA low for ~4.5 YOE, or aligned with current market?
3. Should I take this for better learning/exposure or wait and prepare for stronger companies?

Goal:

- Move towards strong product companies
- Improve system design + real-world backend experience

Would appreciate insights from people in gaming/RMG or similar backend-heavy systems.

title: Uber expectations for staff
content:
I recently interviewed at Uber India and expecting offer soon (L5B). I had couple of questions to help me join:

1. How much TC can I expect?
2. What are some good teams with good work especially related to core AI (not just building MCP servers/agent) or Big Data engine (Apache Pinot) optimizations etc? 
3. Also, I don't want crazy expectations of work hours eg > 8 hours/day. Is it common?

Current TC: 1.6 Cr (Google)

#India #Uber #l5b #staff

title: Zomato offer | SDE-I vs SDE-II dilemma + offer negotiation
content:
Hi,

I’m currently working as an SDE-I with ~2.8 YOE and a CTC of 14 LPA with WFH.

I recently received an offer from Zomato:

- Role: SDE-I

- Comp: 25 LPA fixed + 5 LPA variable

- No stocks

- WFO for 5 days

I was honestly expecting an SDE-II role given my experience, so I’m a bit confused about whether this is the right move.


My questions:

- Does it make sense to switch even if the title is the same?

- Any tips on negotiating here?

    - Is it reasonable to ask for a joining bonus or higher fixed?

    - Has anyone successfully negotiated with Zomato recently?

Would really appreciate insights from people who’ve been in a similar situation 🙏

title: Need help
content:
I’ve been noticing a lot of posts about recent hires, especially for candidates with around 2.8 years of experience who are receiving offers. I was laid off about two months ago and, despite putting in effort, I haven’t been able to secure a job yet.

I’ve had a few interview opportunities, but I struggled to clear the system design rounds. I’m working on improving that, but I also wanted to understand the broader approach others are taking.

In general, how are you all applying for roles?

Are recruiters reaching out to you directly?
Or are you applying through job portals and company websites?
Is there any specific strategy or approach that has worked well for you?

I’d really appreciate any advice or guidance you can share, as I’m actively looking and trying to improve my chances.

title: Kotak Mahindra | Walmart | Flipkart | SDE-2
content:
Hi,

Need some advice choosing between 3 offers.

YOE: 2.9 years
Current CTC: 28.5 LPA (Fixed: 22L)

## Offer 1: Kotak Mahindra Bank

* Role: Software Engineering II
* Total Comp: ₹43.6 LPA
* Fixed: ₹36 LPA (33.44 LPA Fixed)
* Variable: ₹3.6 LPA
* Joining Bonus: ₹4L
* Notice Period: **3 months**
* Domain: BFSI / Banking tech

## Offer 2: Flipkart

* Role: SDE II
* Base: ₹29 LPA (INR 21806 PF)
* Bonus: 10% ~ 2.9 LPA (Performance based)
* Stocks: ~₹15.65L value (vesting 4 years, 25% each)
* Joining Bonus: ₹3L
* Constraint: **2-year clawback / lock-in on joining bonus**
* Team: **Cart team, mostly around food business**

## Offer 3: Walmart Global Tech

* Role: SDE III / GenAI-focused
* Base: ₹28 LPA + ₹1.34 LPA (PF)
* Bonus: 20% ~ ₹5.6 LPA
* Stocks: ~₹9.5L annually (Vesting 3 years, starting Year2), For Year1 -> NO stocks (Refreshment stocks of ₹9.5 LPA every year from Year3, again vesting 3 years)
* Joining Bonus: ~₹4L (1 year lock-in)
* Perks: food, cab, reimbursements, strong insurance
* Constraint: **5 days office**
* Team: **GenAI role in pharmacy**

I’m Evaluating: Future switch opportunities after 1.5-2 years & Good WLB if possible.

Is Walmart gonna give really 20% bonus or more/less?
How's the hike in all 3 companies ?
Whar are your thoughts on Flipkart ESOPs ? How much they can earn me post IPO ?

title: Walmart SSE | Need advice
content:
Hi community, I recently received offer from Walmart for SSE, IN4. 

Current CTC:
Base: 25L
RSU: 25L per year 
overall 50L per year. 

Offered:
Base : 32L
Bonus: 6.4L
RSU: 9.5L with 1/3rd vesting in 1st year. 

Overall CTC comes to be around 41L per year which is lesser than my current CTC. 
HR is not budging for more saying its is 30% hike on base and not willing to give more. 

I know this is serious lowball but want to understand from community what should be done here? Is this the range for SSE in walmart? How do I negotiate with HR?

title: Fair Market Value survey
content:
Hello everyone,

As the headline reads, I am looking to gauge a fair salary range for my profile. 

**Background:**
A core engineering graduate from a Tier 1/2 NIT, with overall close to 9 years of experience.

Initially started as a core engineer for the first 4 years. Switched to an automation role and worked there for 4 years (automating manual jobs using python scripts primarily). 

Currently, working as a Python Dev with roles spanning across data engineering, analysis, traditional ML, and primarily modernizing and extending legacy codebases into python systems.

I have decent exposure to Generative AI tech stack essentially through building personal projects. Have a couple of OS contributions to pandas and huggingface. 

**Current CTC:** 21 LPA (SBC)
**Offer In Hand:** 30 LPA (SBC) (GenAI role)

1. Is 30 LPA a fair compensation for my profile in the current market?
2. Which roles/companies should I target for better compensation (if applicable)?

Thanks in advance.

title: Cracked Nielsen (MTS-1 Full Stack), but now comes the real question.
content:
I recently cleared all rounds and was genuinely excited. Coming from a previous CTC of ₹6 LPA, I quoted ₹17 LPA expecting a strong jump. The HR responded that 17 might not be possible and that they would discuss it with the hiring manager. That got me thinking. At what point does negotiation become being lowballed?
How much of a salary jump is actually realistic?
Should candidates stick to their expectations or adjust based on company bands? This stage feels more complex than the interviews themselves. Trying to balance knowing your worth, understanding market realities, and not losing a good opportunity. Would really appreciate perspectives from others who have gone through similar situations.

title: YipitData vs Akamai — which would you pick? I am confused.
content:
Need help choosing between 2 offers (India, 3.3 (2.9 FTE + 6 Month Internship ) YOE)

Hi folks, I’m a 26 y/o backend engineer trying to decide between (SDE-2):

    YipitData — ₹25 LPA fixed (remote, data-focused, smaller company)

    Akamai — ₹22.8 LPA base + ~10% performance bonus (well-known, more stable)

I’m optimizing for long-term growth (strong backend/system design skills + future top-tier companies).

YipitData seems better for pay + ownership, but Akamai has brand + stability.

I have asked Akamai to make my base to 25 + 10% performance bonus

Which one would you choose and why? Any insights on growth, learning, or exit opportunities would really help.

Thanks!
